creator: saenara@saenara.ru
text:
Fresh FreeBSD 9.1 amd64 installation, right after successfull freebsd-update fetch && freebsd-update install && reboot.
Tryng to fetch ports with portsnap:

---
root@foggy:/root # portsnap --debug fetch
Looking up portsnap.FreeBSD.org mirrors... 6 mirrors found.
pub.ssl                                       100% of  451  B 3972 kBps
done.
Fetching snapshot tag from ec2-eu-west-1.portsnap.freebsd.org... 
snapshot.ssl                                  100% of  256  B 1852 kBps
done.
Fetching snapshot metadata... 
7719e39e7b7751c91689858400a3a514488a0d852d194b100% of  527  B 2973 kBps
done.
Fetching snapshot generated at Tue May 28 04:03:52 MSK 2013:
7719e39e7b7751c91689858400a3a514488a0d852d194b100% of   12 MB  930 kBps 00m00s
Extracting snapshot... snap/5746330c5766f9956e0f8314736b070291076d03388b95d7976f35dd17dc6f19.gz: (Empty error message)
tar: Error exit delayed from previous errors.
root@foggy:/root # tar -tzf /var/db/portsnap/7719e39e7b7751c91689858400a3a514488a0d852d194ba42e7d4f8aad8311d1.tgz  > /dev/null
tar: Truncated input file (needed 3072 bytes, only 0 available)
tar: Error exit delayed from previous errors.
---

Problem persists at least two days. But updating already fetched and extracted ports always successful.

Fix: 

Don't know.
How-To-Repeat: Download FreeBSD-9.1-RELEASE-amd64-disc1.iso from freebsd.org.
Install system with completely default settings.
Run freebsd-update fetch && freebsd-update install && reboot.
Run portsnap fetch.
Enjoy :-)
